nav.probeRun: Compose command from discovery, preview generated eBPF C

header.agentHistoryHint: Up to 10 successfully connected agent addresses; pick from the dropdown while typing.
header.filterPlaceholder: Substring; space means match all tokens…

metrics.hintDisconnected: Connect to pull /proc metrics from the machine where the agent runs.
metrics.cpuJiffiesNote: Cumulative jiffies from /proc/stat since boot — not live CPU %. Often HZ=100 so 1 jiffy ≈ 10 ms; in general 1 jiffy = 1/HZ second.

discover.hint: List, then expand a row. Quick actions open “Run” for template break, hook C, or arg watch. Shift+click runs the REPL line in the CLI. Watch needs an active break on the same catalog probe_id.

quick.shiftHint: Click: open “Run” page (preview eBPF, then execute). Shift+click: fill CLI and run immediately. Command is copied to the clipboard.
quick.ariaGroup: Build probe command for selected row
quick.breakUnavailable: Breakpoints only apply to kprobe kernel symbols.
quick.invalidRow: Cannot build this command for the row (invalid format or disconnected).
quick.opensRunPanel: Opens Run — edit full C, then compile or execute.
quick.shiftNeedsComposer: Shift+run needs a REPL line — open Run first (Hook uses compile there).

probeRun.title: Run from discovery
probeRun.empty: Expand a row in Discover and choose Break / Hook / Watch to compose a command here.
probeRun.hookVsBreakHint: Hook: full eBPF C; probe_point comes from SEC in the object. Break: catalog template only (see info break-templates).
probeRun.breakTemplateHint: Break uses built-in templates only; probe_id like kprobe.do_sys_open.
probeRun.watchHint: Watch prints arg columns when the break on this probe_id fires; run break first.
probeRun.hookSourceOnly: Source editor is for hook C only.
probeRun.compileHookOnly: Clang tab is for hook compile-check only.
probeRun.multiLineConsoleClipboard: Full multi-line script copied to clipboard. CLI shows the first line only; run the rest from the Run panel or paste.
probeRun.noTemplate: Nothing to show for this choice.
probeRun.needConnect: Connect to the agent to compile-check.

compile.skipped: Agent has no bpf include path — only source preview.

cmd.title: Command (execute)
cmd.completionHint: Tab inserts the highlighted completion; ↑/↓ to move; click a row to insert. Enter runs the full line.

hookEditor.hintCompile: Full C with SEC("…"); probe_point is derived from the object. Ringbuf map required. Same as REPL hook attach / gRPC CompileAndAttach.

sessionPanel.title: Session probes
sessionPanel.hintRepl: break / tbreak: catalog probe_id + optional --filter. hook attach: --source|--file; SEC selects probe_point. watch: --sec probe_id [--args].

tabHint.break: Template break: catalog probe_id + optional kernel filter DSL. Commands: break, tbreak, condition, delete, enable, disable.
tabHint.templates: Built-in probe_id list from the agent (info break-templates).
tabHint.hook: hook attach: full eBPF C; probe_point from ELF SEC. Commands: hook attach, hook list, hook delete.
tabHint.watch: Arg-column watch on break hits (EVENT_TYPE_WATCH_ARG). Commands: watch --sec …, delete.

hookMaps.title: BPF maps (loaded program)
hookMaps.hint: Uses hook id from info hook (hook attach or break load). Read-only hex dump of map entries.

events.bufferCaption: This list is the desktop buffer (keeps up to {{max}} rows). Kernel ringbuf and agent stream are separate — see docs.
events.clearBufferAria: Clear the desktop event list buffer
events.clearBufferTitle: Remove all rows from this list only. Does not flush the kernel ringbuf or agent-side channels. Cap ≈ {{max}} rows.
events.emptyDisconnected: Not connected. Connect to the agent, then click Start capture to see events here.
events.emptyNeedCapture: Connected, but capture is off. Click Start capture in the header to subscribe to the event stream.
events.emptyNoProbesYet:
Capture is on. If this stays empty, attach probes first:
• CLI: break <probe_id>, hook attach, watch --sec …
• Hook editor: full C compile & attach
The kernel must actually hit those probes.

settings.title: Settings
settings.hintShortcut: Open this dialog anytime with ⌘ or Ctrl + comma (also under the View menu). Appearance and language are saved locally.

about.title: Phantom
about.subtitle: Remote eBPF debugging — desktop client
about.body: Phantom is a distributed debugger for Linux eBPF: a desktop client connects to a Phantom agent over the network to attach probes, run a GDB-style command interface, and stream structured events from the machine where the agent runs. This application is open source; contributions and feedback are welcome on GitHub.
about.licenseDetail: This software is licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0. You may obtain a copy of the License below. Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS, WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
about.repositoryHint: Browse source, report issues, and follow project updates on GitHub.

glossary.loadavg_one: /proc/loadavg field 1: 1-minute load average (runnable + uninterruptible tasks, interpret per CPU count).
glossary.mem_total_kb: /proc/meminfo MemTotal: total physical memory reported by the kernel (kB).
glossary.mem_available_kb: /proc/meminfo MemAvailable: estimate of memory available for new workloads without swapping (includes reclaimable cache).
glossary.mem_buffers_kb: /proc/meminfo Buffers: raw block I/O cache (usually small).
glossary.mem_cached_kb: /proc/meminfo Cached: page cache and reclaimable slab; large values are often normal.
glossary.cpu_user: /proc/stat: time in user mode for non-niced processes (jiffies).
glossary.cpu_system: /proc/stat: system/kernel mode jiffies (excluding some interrupt accounting).
glossary.cpu_iowait: /proc/stat: time waiting for I/O (may be zero on some kernels/setups).
glossary.rx_bytes: /proc/net/dev: received bytes on the interface (since boot, cumulative).
glossary.vm_size_kb: /proc/<tid>/status VmSize: virtual address space size (VSZ), includes unmapped pages.
glossary.vm_rss_kb: /proc/<tid>/status VmRSS: resident set size mapped to physical memory (approx. RSS).
glossary.threads_count: /proc/<tid>/status Threads: number of tasks in the thread group (often identical on each TID line).
glossary.tid: Thread ID (kernel task id). TGID is the POSIX process ID; TIDs sharing a TGID are threads in one process.
glossary.tgid: Thread group ID: the traditional PID; matches tgid in BPF events.
